text: The patient is positioned in the right lateral decubitus position. The surgeon accesses the abdomen using a paraumbilical Hassan entry and places two additional 5mm ports slightly left of midline in the subxiphoid and lower midline areas. The laparoscopic view initially shows the prominent left psoas muscle obstructing the descending colon. Retraction is used to elevate the descending colon from behind the psoas muscle. Dissection along the white line of Toldt is performed to medialize the descending colon, without mobilizing the splenic flexure.

The surgeon dissects the peritoneum of the left lateral abdominal wall to visualize the nerves, ensuring they are not injured during mesh fixation. A 15x15 cm polyester composite mesh is positioned over the hernia defect and secured to the lateral abdominal wall beneath the peritoneum using absorbable tacks. Medially, the mesh is affixed to the anterior psoas and dorotus fascia with fibrin sealant, leaving the ureter uncovered.
The surgeon repositions the descending colon back to its anatomical location after the laparoscopic repair of the psoas hernia.
